assets.abstract: Second-person neuroscience focuses on studying the behavioral and neuronal mechanisms of real-time social interactions within single and across interacting brains. In this review article, we describe the developments that have been undertaken to study socially interactive phenomena and the behavioral and neurobiological processes that extend across interaction partners. More specifically, we focus on the role that synchrony across brains plays in enabling and facilitating social interaction and

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.
